rules.rule: You MUST ONLY use blocks listed below - NO OTHER BLOCKS EXIST
You MUST follow the EXACT XML structure shown for each block
Block IDs must only contain: letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens (NO special characters like (){}[]/#!)
All value inputs MUST use <shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">value</field></shadow>
NEVER invent new block types or modify existing block structures
For Stop Loss and Take Profit, ALWAYS use trade_entry_price block with ATR-based offsets for proper risk management
Stop Loss pattern: operator_subtract(trade_entry_price, ATR * multiplier) - Use ATR for volatility-adjusted stops
Take Profit pattern: operator_add(trade_entry_price, ATR * multiplier * risk_reward_ratio) - Use 2:1 or 3:1 risk-reward ratios
INDUSTRY STANDARD: Use ATR (Average True Range) with 14-period for dynamic stop losses that adapt to market volatility
RISK MANAGEMENT: Stop Loss = 1.5-2.5x ATR from entry | Take Profit = Stop Loss distance * 2-3 (risk-reward ratio)
NEVER use control_wait, control_wait_until, or control_repeat_until blocks. These cause infinite loops in the Strategy Tester. ALWAYS use control_if with environment_new_candle_open for timing logic.
ALWAYS set the "SIZE" field to 0.1 in trade_order blocks unless explicitly instructed otherwise. THIS IS CRITICAL. NEVER USE 100.

TIMEFRAME: ALL timeframe fields (for both environment blocks and indicators) MUST use minute values. NEVER use string codes like '1h' or '1d'. USE THIS MAPPING TABLE: - "1 Minute" -> "1" - "5 Minutes" -> "5" - "15 Minutes" -> "15" - "30 Minutes" -> "30" - "1 Hour" -> "60" - "4 Hours" -> "240" - "1 Day" -> "1440" - "1 Week" -> "10080" - "1 Month" -> "43200" ALWAYS set this to the user's requested timeframe (default to 60 if unspecified).
Use ta_highest and ta_lowest blocks for finding highest/lowest values over a period.
For Donchian and Keltner blocks, the 'shift' attribute in mutation MUST be a positive integer (>= 1). NEVER use 0.
For ALL indicator blocks (including VidYa, AMA, etc.), you MUST explicitly set the 'PERIOD' field to the requested timeframe (in minutes). DO NOT rely on defaults.

CROSSOVER STRATEGIES: When comparing two indicators of the SAME type (e.g., SMA vs SMA, EMA vs EMA): - ALWAYS use DIFFERENT settings in the <mutation> element (ma_period, shift, etc.) - For moving average crossovers: use Fast (shorter period) vs Slow (longer period) - Standard patterns: Fast SMA (ma_period="10") vs Slow SMA (ma_period="20") - Standard patterns: Fast EMA (ma_period="12") vs Slow EMA (ma_period="26") - Set the NAME field to reflect the difference: "Fast SMA" vs "Slow SMA" - NEVER compare two identical indicators - this makes NO logical sense for trading

template.name: RSI Oversold Reversal (Simple)
template.description: Buy when RSI drops below 30 (oversold), sell when it rises above 70 (overbought). Standard 14-period RSI mean reversion strategy.

template.name: Simple MA Crossover (Intermediate)
template.description: A simple Moving Average crossover strategy. Uses SMA(12) with Shift(6). Enters on bar open if price crosses the MA. Reverses on opposite signal.

template.name: SMA vs SMA Crossover (Golden Cross)
template.description: Classic dual moving average crossover. Buy when Fast SMA (10) crosses above Slow SMA (20). This demonstrates using TWO indicators of the same type with DIFFERENT parameters.

template.name: Bollinger Band Breakout (Intermediate)
template.description: Enter trades when price breaks above upper band (bullish) or below lower band (bearish). Standard 20-period, 2-deviation volatility breakout strategy.

template.name: MACD Momentum (Intermediate)
template.description: Buy when MACD crosses above signal line with ADX>25 trend confirmation. Standard MACD(12,26,9) momentum strategy.

template.name: VWAP Scalping (Advanced)
template.description: Quick scalping strategy: buy when price is below VWAP with RSI(9)<40. Tight stops for high-frequency trading.

template.name: Triple EMA Trend (Advanced)
template.description: Advanced trend following with three EMAs (8,21,55 Fibonacci periods). Enter when fast > medium > slow (aligned trend). Strong trend filter.
